description: §55.10 Level 4 — the `<match for=ValidationError on=@field.errors[0]>` escape hatch RENDERS the developer-controlled arm string. Typing "A" fails length(>=2); `@signup.name.errors[0]` is the LengthFailed ValidationError, so the `<LengthFailed : "Name is too short">` arm renders (bypassing messageFor entirely). This is the kickstarter-v2 §6 'full control' pattern.
rationale: §55.10 Level 4 (total control at the call site, §18.0.1 block-form match): the developer bypasses the messageFor resolution chain and dispatches on the ValidationError variant directly. The arm body is DEVELOPER-AUTHORED and normative, asserted verbatim. Pre-fix (msgchain-render-wiring-2026-07-03) this crashed at module init: `@signup.name.errors[0]` lowered to `_scrml_reactive_get("signup").name.errors[0]` (a nested read on the DERIVED compound → undefined → `[0]` threw), the Shape-B dispatch effect ran before the synth cell was declared, and the dispatcher extracted the tag via `.variant` (ValidationError keys on `.tag`). All three are fixed. The `<match>` renders the raw arm string into its mount slot (no `.scrml-error` wrapper — that structure is a `<errors of=>` concern), so the assertion reads `#err` textContent.
